steps.title: Use the Bronze Age dates
steps.content: The passage says British metalsmiths began developing swords in the Middle Bronze Age, around the 14th to 10th centuries BCE. The Hollingbourne hoard dates to the 10th century BCE, while the Arreton Down hoard dates earlier, to the 17th century BCE.

steps.title: Reject misplaced sword evidence
steps.content: Choice A says both hoards had sword-production evidence, which the timing does not explain. Choice B focuses on modern discovery dates, not artifact dates. Choice C puts evidence in the earlier hoard and then discusses intact swords instead. The answer is D.
